name: Ron DeSantis
summary: Ron DeSantis's character and pattern of behavior show a consistent effort to restrict individual rights, particularly for marginalized groups such as women, LGBTQ+ individuals, and immigrants. He has repeatedly supported and signed legislation that limits access to abortion, targets transgender people, and restricts voting rights. DeSantis has also demonstrated a willingness to use divisive rhetoric and take actions that can be perceived as racist, such as telling voters not to 'monkey this up' by choosing a black candidate. His actions and policies often align with those of former President Trump, and he has shown a tendency to prioritize partisan loyalty over the well-being of his constituents, as seen in his handling of the COVID-19 pandemic and his recruitment of asylum seekers to be flown to other states. Overall, DeSantis's behavior suggests a commitment to a conservative ideology that often comes at the expense of vulnerable populations.

entries.fact: DeSantis voted in favor of H.R. 45, which would have repealed the Affordable Care Act
DeSantis signed a 'No Climate Tax Pledge' against any tax hikes to fight global warming
DeSantis introduced a bill that would have required the Justice Department to report to Congress whenever any federal agency refrained from enforcing laws
DeSantis co-sponsored Kate's Law, which would have increased penalties for aliens who unlawfully reenter the U.S. after being removed
DeSantis introduced the Higher Education Reform and Opportunity Act, which would have allowed states to create their own accreditation systems
DeSantis received a '0' rating from the Human Rights Campaign on LGBT-related legislation
DeSantis proposed legislation that would have ended funding for the Mueller investigation of President Trump
DeSantis voted for the 2017 Trump tax cuts
DeSantis told Florida voters not to 'monkey this up' by choosing Andrew Gillum, a comment that was criticized as racist
DeSantis signed a measure that would make it harder to launch successful ballot initiatives
DeSantis signed an anti-'sanctuary city' bill into law, requiring local governments to aid federal immigration-enforcement efforts

DeSantis announced his support for eliminating ballot drop boxes and limiting voting by mail by requiring that voters re-register every year to vote by mail and that signatures on mail-in ballots 'match the most recent signature on file'
DeSantis announced support for legislation to hold tech companies accountable to prevent alleged political censorship
DeSantis and other Florida Republicans pushed legislation to prohibit tech companies from de-platforming political candidates in response to social media networks removing Trump from their platforms

DeSantis signed the Fairness in Women's Sports Act, banning transgender girls and women from participating and competing in middle-school and high-school girls' and college women's sports competitions
DeSantis led an effort to ban the teaching of critical race theory in Florida public schools, describing it as 'teaching kids to hate their country'
DeSantis announced a new bill, the Stop Wrongs to Our Kids and Employees Act, which would allow parents to sue school districts that teach critical race theory
DeSantis voiced support for the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act, which prohibits discussion of sexual orientation or gender identity in school classrooms from kindergarten to grade 3

Ron DeSantis signed into law a bill that bans abortions after 15 weeks of pregnancy, with exceptions for physical health risks or fatal fetal abnormalities, but no exceptions for rape, human trafficking, or incest
Ron DeSantis responded to Charlie Crist's question about serving a full four-year term by saying 'the only worn-out old donkey I'm looking to put out to pastures is Charlie Crist'

DeSantis signed a six-week abortion ban with exceptions for rape, incest, or human trafficking, but requires the woman to provide proof of a crime before being permitted an abortion under any of those exceptions
The six-week abortion ban legislation makes providing an abortion a felony punishable by up to five years in prison, bans telemedicine for abortion, and limits the availability of medication abortion
DeSantis signed into law a bill that requires social media platforms to prohibit people under 16 years old from making accounts
DeSantis signed a bill requiring government employers and contractors to use E-Verify
DeSantis sidelined scientists and followed Trump during the COVID-19 pandemic, potentially causing harm to the public
DeSantis announced legislation to crack down on big tech and online censorship, which could potentially harm free speech
DeSantis signed into law the Combating Public Disorder Act, which forbids intimidation by mobs and penalizes damage to historic properties or memorials
DeSantis signed Florida's anti-riot bill
Florida joined states to ban transgender girls from sports
Florida board votes to ban critical race theory from state classrooms under DeSantis' administration
DeSantis pushes bill targeting critical race theory in schools
DeSantis pitches 'Stop WOKE Act' to banish perceived influence of critical race theory from schools and workplaces
DeSantis signed a bill creating an election police unit to investigate election fraud
Ron DeSantis signaled support for the 'Don't Say Gay' bill
DeSantis signed the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act into law, which took effect on July 1, 2022
Gov. Ron DeSantis signed Florida's 'Don't Say Gay' bill into law
DeSantis signed a bill eliminating Disney's special independent district act and replacing its Disney-appointed board of overseers
Ron DeSantis signed a bill banning abortions after 15 weeks
DeSantis's administration recruited 50 newly arrived asylum seekers in San Antonio, Texas, and flew them to Martha's Vineyard, Massachusetts
DeSantis's administration recruited 50 newly arrived asylum seekers in San Antonio, Texas, and flew them to Martha's Vineyard, Massachusetts
Ron DeSantis signed a six-week abortion ban into law
DeSantis refused to accept $346 million from the Inflation Reduction Act for rebates to homeowners who want to retrofit their houses and make them more energy efficient
DeSantis vetoed a bill that would have created a statewide process to issue closures and send warnings if the bacteria in waterways reached unsafe levels
DeSantis signed a bill eliminating Walt Disney World's Reedy Creek district
DeSantis signed a bill creating an election police unit
Venezuelan migrants sued DeSantis for flying them to Martha's Vineyard 'under false pretenses'
DeSantis announced plans to expand the 'Don't Say Gay' law to all grades
DeSantis signed a controversial bill targeting local illegal immigration
DeSantis announced he would send Florida National Guard soldiers to Texas for border security
